examples.title: Generate Three-Address Code for Expressions
examples.description: Algorithm to convert infix expression to TAC using stack
examples.code:
ALGORITHM GenerateTAC(expr)
BEGIN
 FOR each token in expr DO
 IF token is operand THEN
 push(token)
 ELSE IF token is operator THEN
 op2 = pop()
 op1 = pop()
 temp = newTemp()
 emit(temp + '=' + op1 + token + op2)
 push(temp)
 END IF
 END FOR
 RETURN result
END
examples.explanation: Uses stack to evaluate expression left-to-right. Operands pushed, operators pop two operands, create temporary, emit TAC instruction, push result back.

examples.title: Infix to Postfix Conversion
examples.description: Shunting-yard algorithm for converting infix to postfix
examples.code:
ALGORITHM InfixToPostfix(infix)
BEGIN
 stack = empty
 output = empty
 precedence = {'+':1, '-':1, '*':2, '/':2}
 
 FOR each token in infix DO
 IF token is operand THEN
 output.append(token)
 ELSE IF token is '(' THEN
 stack.push(token)
 ELSE IF token is ')' THEN
 WHILE stack.top != '(' DO
 output.append(stack.pop())
 END WHILE
 stack.pop()
 ELSE
 WHILE stack.notEmpty AND precedence[token] <= precedence[stack.top] DO
 output.append(stack.pop())
 END WHILE
 stack.push(token)
 END IF
 END FOR
 
 WHILE stack.notEmpty DO
 output.append(stack.pop())
 END WHILE
 
 RETURN output
END
examples.explanation: Uses operator precedence and stack to reorder tokens. Higher precedence operators appear later in postfix output.

examples.title: Build AST from Postfix Expression
examples.description: Stack-based algorithm to construct AST from postfix notation
examples.code:
ALGORITHM BuildAST(postfix)
BEGIN
 stack = empty
 
 FOR each token in postfix DO
 IF token is operand THEN
 node = CreateNode(token, NULL, NULL)
 stack.push(node)
 ELSE IF token is operator THEN
 right = stack.pop()
 left = stack.pop()
 node = CreateNode(token, left, right)
 stack.push(node)
 END IF
 END FOR
 
 RETURN stack.pop()
END
examples.explanation: Operand creates leaf node, operator pops two nodes, creates internal node with them as children, pushes result tree back.

examples.title: DAG Construction for Expressions
examples.description: Algorithm to build DAG by detecting common subexpressions
examples.code:
ALGORITHM BuildDAG(expr)
BEGIN
 nodeTable = empty hashmap
 dagNodes = empty list
 
 FOR each subexpression in expr DO
 key = subexpression operator + children keys
 
 IF key exists in nodeTable THEN
 existingNode = nodeTable[key]
 addParent(existingNode, currentPosition)
 ELSE
 newNode = CreateDAGNode(subexpression)
 dagNodes.append(newNode)
 nodeTable[key] = newNode
 END IF
 END FOR
 
 RETURN dagNodes
END
examples.explanation: Hashmap stores expression signatures; if subexpression already exists, reuse the node instead of creating duplicate.
